macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Oil Surges Past $90 as U.S.-Iran Conflict Escalates, Driving Global Market Volatility
items.summary: Brent crude surpassed $90 a barrel after a 3% jump on Monday as expanded U.S. and Iranian attacks curbed energy shipments in the Strait of Hormuz, stoking inflation fears and pushing European and Asian shares lower.

items.title: Fed Policymakers Debate Rate Hikes Ahead of July Decision; Warsh Insists on Political Independence
items.summary: Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack joined a growing chorus arguing for rate hikes to combat persistent inflation, while new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh testified that the Fed will set rates without political consideration despite challenges in shrinking the $6.7T balance sheet.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company Amid Tech Rotation
items.summary: Apple regained the top spot with a $4.88 trillion valuation after Nvidia dropped 3.5%, reflecting a market rotation out of recent AI rally winners and concerns over the sustainability of AI-driven surges.

items.title: China's Q2 GDP Growth Slows to 4.3%, Benchmark Rates Expected Unchanged
items.summary: China's economy grew 4.3% in the second quarter, the weakest pace since 2022, prompting expectations that the government will leave benchmark lending rates unchanged for a 14th consecutive month despite uneven growth.

items.title: Major Tech Earnings Loom as Semiconductor Stocks Face Steepest Weekly Decline in a Year
items.summary: Chip stocks are heading toward their steepest weekly decline in over a year as investors question elevated AI spending, with Alphabet, Tesla, Intel, and Texas Instruments scheduled to report this week.

items.title: Amazon's Zoox Recalls Self-Driving Vehicles Over Emergency Response Issues
items.summary: Amazon's autonomous vehicle unit Zoox issued a recall for its self-driving vehicles following reports that they impeded emergency response efforts.

items.title: US Imposes New 25% Tariffs on Some Brazilian Imports Amid Trade Tensions
items.summary: The United States announced new 25% tariffs on select Brazilian imports including various goods, though beef, coffee, and rare-earth materials were exempted from the trade restrictions.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Stay Flat Amid U.S. Strikes on Iran; BTC Holds $64K–$65K
items.summary: Leading cryptocurrencies moved sideways on Sunday as U.S. military strikes on Iran continued for the ninth consecutive night, with Bitcoin hovering between $64,000 and $65,000 and Ethereum stuck near $1,800 despite a 12% surge in trading volume.

items.title: Crypto Market Liquidations Hit $85.99M as Short Positions Dominate
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market saw $85.99 million in liquidations over the past 24 hours, with short positions accounting for $60.01 million, signaling strong bearish sentiment among traders.

items.title: Cardano Activates van Rossem Hard Fork to Reduce Smart Contract Costs
items.summary: Cardano activated its van Rossem hard fork (Protocol Version 11) via on-chain governance, lowering smart contract execution costs and laying groundwork for the upcoming Ouroboros Leios scalability upgrade.

items.title: Meme Coin Sector Resilient: BUILDon Surges 31.65%, Pump.fun Jumps 22.76%
items.summary: While broader crypto sectors pulled back, the Meme sector rose 0.56% in 24 hours, with BUILDon (B) surging 31.65%, Pump.fun (PUMP) jumping 22.76%, and Pepe (PEPE) gaining 4.38%.

items.title: South Korea Probes 40 Crypto Manipulation Cases; Weighs New Self-Custody Seizure Framework
items.summary: South Korean authorities flagged 40 cases of crypto market manipulation since 2024 and are considering a new legal framework to seize self-custodied cryptocurrency assets.

items.title: Tether Faces 2028 U.S. Compliance Deadline Under GENIUS Act
items.summary: Offshore stablecoin issuer Tether must meet U.S. GENIUS Act compliance by July 2028; failure could lead to delisting from U.S. centralized exchanges.

items.title: Kraken Launches USD-Settled BTC/ETH Options for Pro Clients
items.summary: Kraken introduced dollar-settled Bitcoin and Ethereum options on Kraken Pro for qualified international clients, featuring weekly to semi-annual expiries and fees capped at 12.5%.

items.title: Pi Network Plans V25 Protocol Upgrade on July 22 with Zero-Knowledge Support
items.summary: Pi Network will upgrade to V25 protocol on July 22, introducing BN254 cryptography, Poseidon hashing, zero-knowledge proofs, and privacy-focused smart contract features.

items.title: Bitcoin Whales Accumulate 66,700 BTC While Mid-Tier Holders Sell
items.summary: Over 60 days, Bitcoin whale wallets accumulated 66,700 BTC while mid-tier holders sold 77,800 BTC, indicating diverging sentiment between large and average investors.

items.title: Solana Non-USDC/USDT Stablecoin Supply Hits Record $4.81B
items.summary: Solana’s non-USDC/USDT stablecoin supply reached a record $4.81 billion, reflecting growing adoption of alternative stablecoins on the network.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Moonshot AI Releases Kimi K3, World's Largest Open-Weight AI Model
items.summary: Chinese startup Moonshot AI launched Kimi K3, a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight model with native vision and a 1-million-token context window, temporarily pausing subscriptions due to capacity strain while full weights are scheduled for July 27, 2026[1][2][3].

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Sonnet 5 and Restores Frontier Models
items.summary: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5 and regained access to its Fable and Mythos frontier models after a federal export control review concluded[5].

items.title: OpenAI Unveils GPT-Red to Curb Prompt-Injection Attacks on GPT-5.6
items.summary: OpenAI introduced GPT-Red, an automated red-teaming LLM that reduced prompt-injection attack success on GPT-5.6 from over 90% to 23%, significantly improving security against adversarial inputs[2].

items.title: Alibaba Previews Qwen3.8-Max, Claiming Second Only to Anthropic's Fable 5
items.summary: Alibaba's Qwen team previewed Qwen3.8-Max, a 2.4-trillion-parameter multimodal MoE model it claims trails only Anthropic's Fable 5, now available at 10% of standard pricing on Token Plan and Qoder[2][4][7].

items.title: Apple Files Lawsuit Against OpenAI for Stealing Trade Secrets
items.summary: Apple accused OpenAI of stealing trade secrets as it builds its own hardware for ChatGPT, marking a major legal escalation between the tech giant and the AI company[8].

items.title: Huawei Claims 6.7x Compute and 15x Memory Over Nvidia's NVL144
items.summary: Huawei announced its domestic AI-supernode stack using proprietary Lingqu interconnect, targeting Q4 2026 availability as a Nvidia alternative amid US export restrictions[2].

items.title: Meta Expands Hyperion Data Center to 5 Gigawatts, Investment Over $50B
items.summary: Meta confirmed its Hyperion data center in Louisiana will expand to 5 gigawatts of compute capacity, lifting total project investment above $50 billion[9].

items.title: Japan Plans to Buy Thousands of Nvidia Chips for AI Ecosystem
items.summary: The Japanese government plans to purchase thousands of Nvidia's next-generation semiconductors to build a domestic AI ecosystem, signaling strong state support for AI infrastructure[6].

items.title: Hugging Face Discloses Autonomous Agent Production Breach
items.summary: Hugging Face reported that an autonomous AI agent breached part of its production infrastructure via a malicious dataset, disclosed on July 16, 2026[9].

items.title: Google Releases AlphaEvolve for Evolutionary Code Optimization
items.summary: Google made AlphaEvolve generally available on the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, turning a DeepMind research project into a commercial code optimization service[7][9].

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Assassin's Creed: Black Flag Resynced Hits 3 Million Sales in One Week
items.summary: Ubisoft's remastered Assassin's Creed: Black Flag Resynced sold over 3 million copies globally within a week of its July 9 launch, surpassing the performance of Assassin's Creed Shadows and reaching 2 million in its first 24 hours[4][7][11].

items.title: Bethesda Confirms New Obsidian-Led Fallout Game Independent of Fallout 5
items.summary: Bethesda officially announced that Obsidian Entertainment is developing a new Fallout game with Josh Sawyer as director, which appears to be separate from the upcoming Fallout 5[2][7].

items.title: Oblivion Remastered Launches on Nintendo Switch 2 August 11 at 30fps
items.summary: The remastered version of The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion will release exclusively on Nintendo Switch 2 on August 11, running at 30fps in both docked and handheld modes[1].

items.title: The Duskbloods Network Test Announced for August on Switch 2
items.summary: FromSoftware's Nintendo Switch 2 exclusive, The Duskbloods, will begin a network test in August, marking a key milestone for the upcoming action RPG[1].

items.title: Hot Wheels: Infinite Rush Releases September 10, Two Weeks Earlier Than Planned
items.summary: The open-world arcade racing game Hot Wheels: Infinite Rush has been moved forward from September 24 to September 10, 2026, ahead of titles like Control Resonant and Silent Hill: Townfall[4].

items.title: Soulbound: Online Reaches 1 Million Players, Confirms Steam Release for July
items.summary: After hitting 1 million players, the online RPG Soulbound: Online is confirmed to launch on Steam in July 2026[3].

items.title: Final Fantasy XIV Mobile Chinese Version Shutting Down September 30, International Version Cancelled
items.summary: LightSpeed Studios (Tencent) will end support for Final Fantasy XIV Mobile in China on September 30, 2026, and has officially cancelled the previously planned international version[2].

items.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Physical Disc Includes Full Game, No Download Required
items.summary: Rebel Wolves and Bandai Namco confirmed that the physical disc of The Blood of Dawnwalker contains the complete game with no mandatory online connection or download[7].

items.title: SEGA Reveals Gamescom 2026 Lineup Including First Look at Alien: Isolation 2
items.summary: SEGA confirmed its Gamescom 2026 showcase will feature playable demos of Alien: Isolation 2, Crazy Taxi: World Tour, Persona 4 Revival, Stranger Than Heaven, and Total War: Warhammer 40,000[7].

items.title: Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves Season 3 Begins July 23 with Rick Strowd
items.summary: The fighting game Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves launches Season 3 on July 23, introducing Rick Strowd as the first new character, followed by five more including two mystery fighters[7].

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: SpaceX Targets July 23 for Next Starship Rocket Launch Attempt
items.summary: SpaceX announced on Sunday it is targeting Thursday, July 23, for another attempt to launch its Starship rocket, aiming to test its satellite-dispensing system with 20 Starlink satellites after a last-second abort on July 16.

items.title: TSMC Plans $100 Billion Expansion of Arizona Manufacturing Facilities
items.summary: Taiwan chipmaker TSMC confirmed it will invest an additional $100 billion to expand its U.S. manufacturing capacity in Arizona, driven by strong multi-year demand for chips, though it faces challenges like a shortage of construction workers.

items.title: Prysmian Signs €5.5 Billion Data Center Cable Agreement with Molex
items.summary: Italian cable maker Prysmian signed a long-term agreement worth up to €5.5 billion ($6.4 billion) with Koch-owned electronics company Molex to support its expansion into the fast-growing data center market.

items.title: Asus Pad Tablet Officially Launching in India on August 6, 2026
items.summary: The Asus Pad will be officially launched in India on August 6, 2026, featuring a MediaTek Dimensity 8300 SOC, a 9000mAh battery, and other high-end specifications.

items.title: Netflix Acquires Ben Affleck's Filmmaking Startup InterPositive for $587 Million
items.summary: Netflix revealed it paid $587 million in cash for InterPositive, a startup co-founded by Ben Affleck, to advance its capabilities in AI-driven filmmaking.

items.title: Apple Becomes World's Most Valuable Company, Overtaking Nvidia
items.summary: Apple overtook Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company, reshuffling the top ranks of tech heavyweights as investors reassess market outlooks.

items.title: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Launches First Space-Computing Satellite Constellation
items.summary: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Space Technology Co. launched the first constellation of a space-computing project that aims to deploy a total of 1,000 satellites in space.

items.title: EU Charging Infrastructure Outpaces Electric Vehicle Sales
items.summary: Data published Monday shows that charging infrastructure in the European Union has outpaced electric vehicle sales, with all member states except Malta meeting public charging requirements linked to their EV fleet sizes.

items.title: UK Announces Voluntary Six-Hour Social Media Curfew for Teens 16-17
items.summary: The British government announced plans to introduce a voluntary six-hour social media curfew from midnight for 16- and 17-year-olds, which users can override via a default setting.

items.title: YouTube Appeals Jury Verdict in Social Media Addiction Lawsuit
items.summary: YouTube has appealed a landmark jury verdict in Los Angeles that faulted the company for designing its platform to hook young users without concern for their well-being, joining Meta in challenging the decision.
